methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rebellion Defense is a Defense & Security company at the SPAC stage that has raised $150M to date. The valuation universe comprises 10 identified comparables: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers spanning the sector, with top-tier private benchmarks including Anduril Industries, True Anomaly, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety.

Standard peer-median valuation proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. A search across same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier filters yielded fewer than three datapoints necessary for robust statistical inference, despite 21 total Defense & Security peers existing in the broader universe. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable selection bias given the heterogeneity of business models and customer acquisition dynamics across the Defense & Security landscape.

The analysis therefore cannot support a point valuation or tight range without additional primary data collection—either from direct management discussions, customer pipeline validation, or targeted benchmarking against the identified public and late-stage private comparables. Key uncertainties include Rebellion Defense's revenue scale relative to Anduril and True Anomaly, customer concentration risk, and the pace of adoption in its specific end-market segment. Future valuation work should prioritize gathering margin, growth rate, and go-to-market efficiency metrics from accessible peers to enable credible triangulation.
